sourceMovie.title: The War of the Roses
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit The War of the Roses for the same reason it broke through in 1989: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a drama rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Danny DeVito keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The comedy and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Crazy, Stupid, Love.
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The War of the Roses clicked for you, Crazy, Stupid, Love. (2011) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and drama, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The War of the Roses?
faq.answer: Try Crazy, Stupid, Love., The Proposal, The Lobster—each shares DNA with The War of the Roses while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The War of the Roses?
faq.answer: The War of the Roses (1989) is best described as Comedy, Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The War of the Roses worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.9/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this comedy lane.
